title: Collections should implement the generic interface
description: Collections should implement the generic interfaceAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?The NET Framework 2.0 introduced the generic interfaceSystem.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<T>and it should be preferred over the older, non generic, interfaces.This rule raises an issue when a public type implementsSystem.Collections.IEnumerable.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; using System.Collections; public class MyData { public MyData() { } } public class MyList : CollectionBase // Noncompliant { public void Add(MyData data) { InnerList.Add(data); } // ... }Compliant solutionusing System; using System.Collections.ObjectModel; public class MyData { public MyData() { } } public class MyList : Collection<MyData> { // Implementation... }Available I
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
